oneLiner: A mobile app that turns WhatsApp voice notes from clients into structured tasks, prices, and invoice drafts.
targetUsers: Freelancers who receive work requests through WhatsApp
Tutors, mechanics, designers, cleaners, photographers, and other local service providers
Small service businesses that do not need a full CRM
problem: Many service providers run their business from WhatsApp. Client instructions arrive as voice notes and chat messages, but invoices, task lists, and follow-ups still require manual copying into separate tools.
solution: Let users share a WhatsApp voice note or audio file into the app, transcribe it, extract client name, job details, due date, price, and action items, then generate an editable invoice draft that can be exported as PDF or sent back through WhatsApp.
marketGap: Transcription apps stop at text, invoice apps require manual entry, and CRMs are too heavy for small informal businesses. The gap is a mobile-first workflow from conversation to billable document.
originalValue: The original value is not transcription itself. It is converting informal client communication into a billable business artifact: task list, quote, invoice, and follow-up message in one flow.
revenueOpportunity: This sits between AI productivity, invoicing, and small-business workflow software. Revenue can come from conversion limits, invoice templates, recurring usage, team accounts, and later payment-link or accounting integrations.
whyNow: AI transcription and structured extraction are good enough for practical mobile workflows, while more small businesses are already using WhatsApp as their primary sales and support channel.

evidence.finding: AI assistant apps and workflow-specific AI tools are growing as users move beyond generic chat.
evidence.source: App intelligence and market trend research

evidence.finding: Small service providers commonly use messaging apps as their business inbox.
Small SaaS and mobile apps with clear workflow value are common acquisition targets on app/startup marketplaces.

competitors.name: Generic transcription apps
competitors.weakness: Do not create invoices, tasks, or client records
competitors.opportunity: Own the full workflow after transcription

competitors.name: Invoice maker apps
competitors.weakness: Manual data entry from chats and voice notes
competitors.opportunity: Reduce invoice creation time from minutes to seconds

competitors.name: Small business CRMs
competitors.weakness: Too complex for informal solo providers

goals: Reduce the time from client voice note to invoice draft.
Create a simple mobile workflow for non-technical service providers.
Support repeat usage through client history, templates, and monthly invoice tracking.
mvpFeatures: Import or share audio file into the app
Extract client, service, price, due date, and notes
userStories: As a freelancer, I want to share a client voice note into the app so I can avoid typing the request manually.
As a tutor, I want the app to detect the service and price so I can create an invoice quickly.
As a service provider, I want to edit extracted fields before exporting so I can correct AI mistakes.
As a small business owner, I want invoice history so I can see what I billed this month.
functionalRequirements: The app must accept audio imports from Android share sheet.
The app must show transcription text before invoice generation.
The app must allow users to edit all extracted fields.
The app must generate a PDF invoice.
The app must store invoice drafts locally or in the user's account.
The app must include a disclaimer that AI extraction can be wrong and requires review.
nonFunctionalRequirements: MVP should work on Android first.
Invoice generation should complete within 30 seconds for typical short voice notes.
User data should not be used for training unless explicitly opted in.
The app should support low-end phones and intermittent connectivity where possible.
monetization: Free tier with 10 conversions per month
Monthly subscription for unlimited conversions and invoice templates
One-time paid template pack for local business invoice designs
Future team plan for small service businesses
addOns: Client CRM with job history and repeat-service reminders
Payment links and payment status tracking
Expense receipt capture tied to each invoice
Multi-language voice extraction for local markets
Agency/team workspace for multiple field workers
successMetrics: Activation: first invoice generated within 5 minutes of install
Retention: user generates at least 3 invoices in first 14 days
Conversion: 5 percent free-to-paid conversion after usage limit
Quality: less than 10 percent extraction correction rate for key invoice fields
validationPlan: Interview 10 freelancers or local service providers who use WhatsApp for clients.
Create a landing page with a demo video and collect waitlist signups.
Manually process 20 real or sample voice notes to test field extraction accuracy.
Test pricing with two offers: monthly subscription and one-time invoice pack.
Launch a closed Android beta with 20 users.
risks: Speech-to-text accuracy may be weak for noisy audio or mixed languages.
WhatsApp integration must use share/import workflows rather than unsupported scraping.
Users may distrust invoices generated by AI unless editing and review are clear.
Payment collection may be requested but should remain out of v1 scope.
openQuestions: Which languages and accents should be supported first?
Should the app start with Android only or Flutter cross-platform?
Which invoice formats are required for the first target country?
Do users prefer subscription pricing or pay-per-invoice credits?
